rulings.Case Title: DAVID PHILLIPS VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Sid E. Parker Boiler Mfg. Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's General Order 129 interrogatory responses and Sid Danenhauer's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (handhole gaskets and cabinet door gaskets) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's objections to Sid Danenhauer's declaration and attached exhibits are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and the deposition testimony of Sid Danenhauer with attached exhibits are overruled. The Court did not consider Charles Ay's declaration in making its decision. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID PHILLIPS VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections to plaintiff's Exhibit H are sustained and its objections to plaintiff's Exhibits B, C, D, E, F and I are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Nancy Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Brenda McLain-Condiotti is denied without prejudice to being re-filed upon proper demonstration that the service requirements of CRC 3.1362 were satisfied. Brayton Purcell did not demonstrate that Ms. McLain-Condiotti received proper notice of this motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. rulings.Case Title: ELMO DAVILLIER VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiffs Strict Liability Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's strict liability cause of action is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his strict liability cause of action. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support such claim. This failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's strict liability cause of action. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Dawson's deposition testimony are sustained. However, even if Mr. Dawson's deposition testimony were admissible, it would be insufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
